summary: Decompiled five previously stubbed misc functions across gmregclear, grcastle, groldpupupu, and tydisplay. The PR replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures, exposed just enough unknown struct/global layout to support matching code, and the decomp.dev bot reported 5 new 100% matches totaling +564 matched bytes with no diff regressions.

key_files.role: Decompiled fn_8017DE54 and gm_80180BA0; refined lbl_80472E48_t by exposing x14[0x1B] while preserving sizeof 0x80.
Replaced UNK prototypes with s32 fn_8017DE54(u8, u8*) and void gm_80180BA0(void).
Decompiled fn_801CFAFC as an item/ground callback that writes castle2.xC4 and conditionally calls ftLib_80086A4C.
Added melee item forward declaration and typed fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32).
Added grOp_804D48E0[1][2] and decompiled grOldPupupu_80211C1C with Ground_801C2BA4, PAD_STACK(8), and grAnime_801C8138.
Decompiled un_8031BB34 as an indexed ToyDsp joint-name table with idx -1 normalized to 0.

what_changed.details: Implemented as s32 fn_8017DE54(u8 arg0, u8* arg1). It checks flag bits 0x20, 0x10, and 0x02; for the 0x10 case it compares arg1[0..2] against 0x21 and returns 3 minus the non-0x21 count.
Implemented a loop over 0x1B entries, maps each loop index with gm_80164024, and writes lbl_80472E48.x14[idx] * 0xA through gmMainLib_8015D06C.
Changed a large opaque pad after offset 0x0C into pad_C[0x8] plus s32 x14[0x1B], while retaining STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x80).

Implemented the grcastle function with the callback-like signature Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32. It writes 4 through an s16 cast to gp->gv.castle2.xC4 and, if ftLib_80086960(gobj) is true, calls ftLib_80086A4C(gobj, (f32) grCs_804D6970->x4).

Implemented a stage animation update that reads the current Ground from gobj->user_data, obtains another ground via Ground_801C2BA4(7), checks other_gp->gv.unk.xC8 == 2 and xD4 != 0, then calls grAnime_801C8138 using gp->map_id and grOp_804D48E0[xCC][xD8].
Added static int grOp_804D48E0[1][2] = {{0, 0}}, which the bot also reported as a +14 byte .sdata improvement for groldpupupu.
Implemented an s8-indexed table of ToyDsp *_Top_joint strings, normalizes idx == -1 to 0, and returns the selected pointer cast to s32.

smart_moves: Exposed only the needed lbl_80472E48_t member x14[0x1B] instead of over-naming unknown data, and kept the existing size static_assert to guard layout.
Updated headers at the same time as source decompilation, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact known prototypes and adding the required <melee/it/forward.h> include for Item_GObj.
Preserved callback signatures with currently unused parameters, especially fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32), rather than simplifying away ABI-visible arguments.

Used matching-oriented C constructs where the diff demanded them: PAD_STACK(8), an s16 pointer cast into an unknown ground field, a local char* table returned as s32, and the exact static table shape for grOp_804D48E0.
Kept ambiguous names such as gv.unk.xC8 and gv.castle2.xC4 instead of inventing semantics not supported by the PR evidence.
decomp_lessons: Small unrelated decompilations can be safely grouped when each function is a complete 100% objdiff match and the PR includes build plus ninja diff verification.
For unknown globals, carve out only fields proven by accesses and keep padding plus STATIC_ASSERTs until surrounding code explains the full structure.
Header cleanup is part of decomp progress: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS improves downstream type information and may require forward declarations rather than heavy includes.
Stage code often needs exact Ground gv union offsets and callback signatures; preserve odd casts and unused arguments if they are required for ABI or instruction matching.
When matching data references, include the backing static data object, not just code; grOp_804D48E0 improved groldpupupu .sdata as well as enabling a code match.
Do not force semantic names prematurely for address-named functions or offset-named fields unless the PR evidence identifies their behavior.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions remain address-based with subsystem prefixes: fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, grOldPupupu_80211C1C, un_8031BB34.
Stage globals use stage-specific prefixes plus addresses, such as grOp_804D48E0 and grCs_804D6970.
Unidentified struct members retain offset-style names: pad_C, x14, gv.unk.xC8, gv.unk.xCC, gv.unk.xD4, gv.unk.xD8, and gv.castle2.xC4.
Header declarations keep address comments like /* 17DE54 */ and /* 1CFAFC */ beside function prototypes.

Toy display joint strings use the exact asset naming pattern ToyDsp..._Top_joint, including entries like ToyDspQues_Top_joint, ToyDspMycharaCmA_Top_joint, ToyDspMapA_Top_joint, ToyDspPokemonA_Top_joint, and ToyDspStand_Top_joint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in grOldPupupu_80211C1C to satisfy stack layout.
Used *(s16*) &gp->gv.castle2.xC4 = 4 in fn_801CFAFC to force the observed halfword store into an otherwise unknown field.
Returned (s32) table[idx] from un_8031BB34 even though the table contains char* entries, matching the chosen function return type.
Kept the ToyDsp string table local inside un_8031BB34 and handled the signed s8 sentinel idx == -1 before indexing.
Declared grOp_804D48E0 as static int [1][2] with zero initializer to match both code indexing and .sdata placement.
In gm_80180BA0, the expression calls gm_80164024((u8) i) for the gmMainLib_8015D06C argument even though idx was already computed, a shape consistent with preserving the matched codegen.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 56.90% (+0.01%, +564 bytes) with 5 new 100% matches: grOldPupupu_80211C1C, fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, and un_8031BB34.
Reported one unmatched-item improvement: main/melee/gr/groldpupupu .sdata +14 bytes, from 0.00% to 20.00%.
No human review comments or review requests were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Identify the semantic meaning of lbl_80472E48_t.x14 and whether gm_80180BA0 should eventually receive a non-address-based name.
Trace fn_8017DE54 callers to name the u8 flag bits 0x20, 0x10, and 0x02 and the 0x21 sentinel values in the u8 array.
Confirm the true dimensions and nonzero data, if any, for grOp_804D48E0 and the meanings of other_gp->gv.unk.xC8/xCC/xD4/xD8 in Old Pupupu.
Identify gp->gv.castle2.xC4 and grCs_804D6970->x4 semantics in grcastle before renaming fn_801CFAFC.
Compare un_8031BB34 with nearby tydisplay functions such as un_8031BB94 to determine whether the ToyDsp joint-name index should become an enum or shared table.
